files.contents:
# Hello Colors App

This project is an Express + Tailwind app that serves a small frontend where you can change the displayed "hello world" text color through a UI. The color is stored in memory on the server (so it resets when the server restarts).

Run steps:

1. Install dependencies:

   npm install

2. Build Tailwind CSS once (produces public/dist/styles.css):

   npm run build:css

   Note: For development you can run the `dev` script which will concurrently run nodemon and tailwind in watch mode:

   npm run dev

3. Start the server:

   npm start

4. Open http://localhost:3000 in your browser.

Features:

- Displays "hello world" text.
- Color picker and preset color buttons let you change the text color.
- Changes are sent to the server via POST /api/color and persisted in memory (so they survive page reloads until the server restarts).

Tests:

- Run `npm test` to execute a small automated test that verifies the color API and that the index page is served.

Hello Colors

hello world
Changes are saved to the server and will persist across reloads (until the server restarts).

notes: Build Tailwind CSS with `npm run build:css` if you want the full Tailwind styling; a small fallback CSS is provided at public/dist/styles.css so the app still looks acceptable without building.
Start the server with `npm start`. For development use `npm run dev`.
